## Parameters

The single-cylinder model below is a thin presentation layer over `arty`.
All physics lives in `src/arty/fragmentation.py`; this section only builds
the parameter structs and prints the derived geometry. The default
105 mm M1 HE body here uses the historical TM 9-1901 cylindrical wall
thickness of 11 mm (the four-zone section further down uses the
drawing-derived registry geometry in `arty.shells`).

```{python}
import numpy as np

from arty.fragmentation import (
    ShellParams, DragParams, TargetParams,
    gurney_velocity, mott_params, mott_N, retardation_coeff,
    ke_at_range, min_lethal_mass, pk_given_hit, expected_kills, p_kill,
    _shell_geometry, _PK_E, _PK_VAL,
)

# --- 105 mm M1 HE (single-cylinder model shell) ---
# wall_t = 11 mm (TM 9-1901 cylindrical wall); mass deductions = fuze + band.
shell = ShellParams(
    caliber=0.105, wall_t=0.011,
    mass_total=14.97, mass_filler=2.18, mass_deductions=0.75,
)
drag   = DragParams()      # C_D=0.65, C_shape=0.90, rho_air=1.225
target = TargetParams()    # presented width w = 0.5 m

# Derived geometry / masses from src (SI internally)
r_outer, r_inner, r_bu, mass_shell = _shell_geometry(shell)
r_inner_bu = r_inner * np.sqrt(3)
r_outer_bu = np.sqrt(r_inner_bu**2 + (r_outer**2 - r_inner**2))

# Convenience scalars used by later cells / plots
gurney_const = shell.filler.gurney_const
caliber_m    = shell.caliber
wall_t       = shell.wall_t
mass_total   = shell.mass_total
mass_filler  = shell.mass_filler
rho_steel    = shell.steel.rho
sigma_F      = shell.steel.sigma_f
gamma_mott   = shell.steel.gamma
rho_air      = drag.rho_air
C_D          = drag.C_D
C_shape      = drag.C_shape
w_target     = target.w

print("=== Shell geometry ===")
print(f"  Caliber          : {caliber_m*1e3:.0f} mm")
print(f"  Wall thickness   : {wall_t*1e3:.1f} mm")
print(f"  r_inner (0)      : {r_inner*1e3:.1f} mm")
print(f"  r_inner (bu)     : {r_inner_bu*1e3:.1f} mm  (√3 × initial inner, 3× vol)")
print(f"  r_outer (bu)     : {r_outer_bu*1e3:.1f} mm  (wall volume conserved)")
print(f"  r_mean  (bu)     : {r_bu*1e3:.1f} mm  ← used in Mott formula")
print(f"\n=== Mass ===")
print(f"  Projectile       : {mass_total:.2f} kg")
print(f"  Filler (TNT)     : {mass_filler:.2f} kg")
print(f"  Shell body (M)   : {mass_shell:.2f} kg")
print(f"  M/C ratio        : {mass_shell/mass_filler:.2f}")
```

______________________________________________________________________
